**Coverage notes:** The complete main proceedings and all 35 official workshop-roster events were resolved by a complete paper screen or an exact artifact blocker; nine strict workshop papers were added. Cached official programs closed three former false blockers with zero eligible papers: Democracy and AI screened 5/5 contributed rows, RePSS screened all six challenge Top-3 talks and 3/3 workshop presentations, and LNSAI screened 13/13 paper presentations. Workshop remains omitted from tracks\_checked because exactly three paper rosters are unavailable: (1) STAI, https://stai.jeju.ai/, has no retrievable official 2024 program or accepted-paper roster; (2) IT4PSS, https://wasn.csie.ncu.edu.tw/workshop/IT4PSS2024.html, lacks a complete program/accepted-paper artifact and its official host returns 404; and (3) AIAA, https://ijcai-aiaa-2024.org/, no longer exposes its official 2024 program and redirects to unrelated content.

14 verified papers curated for direct relevance to quantitative finance and asset management. Tracks are separated so main-conference, position, and workshop status remains visible.

[← Back to the main index](../../README.md)

## Main Conference (5)

### [Automatic De-Biased Temporal-Relational Modeling for Stock Investment Recommendation](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/221>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-chen-debiased-stock-recommendation

**Authors:** Weijun Chen, Shun Li, Xipu Yu, Heyuan Wang, Wei Chen, Tengjiao Wang

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling · Portfolio Optimization

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.24963/ijcai.2024/221

**Focus:** Debiases temporal stock relationships before producing investment recommendations aimed at excess returns.

**Why it matters:** Reduces relation-driven selection bias that can make graph-based equity recommendations unstable out of sample.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/221>) · [Paper](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/221>)

---

### [IMM: An Imitative Reinforcement Learning Approach with Predictive Representation Learning for Automatic Market Making](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/663>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-niu-imitative-market-making

**Authors:** Hui Niu, Siyuan Li, Jiahao Zheng, Zhouchi Lin, Bo An, Jian Li, Jian Guo

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Market Microstructure · Execution

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.24963/ijcai.2024/663

**Focus:** Combines imitation and predictive representation learning for automatic quoting at multiple price levels.

**Why it matters:** Learns market-making behavior from demonstrations while adapting quotes to evolving market states.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/663>) · [Paper](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/663>)

---

### [MacMic: Executing Iceberg Orders via Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/664>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-niu-macmic-iceberg-execution

**Authors:** Hui Niu, Siyuan Li, Jian Li

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Execution · Market Microstructure

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.24963/ijcai.2024/664

**Focus:** Splits iceberg-order execution into hierarchical timing and sizing decisions conditioned on limit-order-book states.

**Why it matters:** Controls information leakage and execution cost for large hidden orders across diverse equities.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/664>) · [Paper](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/664>)

---

### [RSAP-DFM: Regime-Shifting Adaptive Posterior Dynamic Factor Model for Stock Returns Prediction](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/676>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-xiang-rsap-dfm

**Authors:** Quanzhou Xiang, Zhan Chen, Qi Sun, Rujun Jiang

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Factor Investing · Alpha Modeling · Market Regimes

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.24963/ijcai.2024/676

**Focus:** Adapts a dynamic stock-return factor model when latent market regimes shift.

**Why it matters:** Lets factor forecasts respond to structural changes rather than averaging incompatible return environments.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/676>) · [Paper](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/676>)

---

### [Trade When Opportunity Comes: Price Movement Forecasting via Locality-Aware Attention and Iterative Refinement Labeling](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/678>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-zeng-locality-aware-trading

**Authors:** Liang Zeng, Lei Wang, Hui Niu, Ruchen Zhang, Ling Wang, Jian Li

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling · Financial Forecasting

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.24963/ijcai.2024/678

**Focus:** Uses locality-aware attention and refined labels to identify tradable price moves across stocks, ETFs, and cryptoassets.

**Why it matters:** Focuses the forecast on opportunities that translate into quantitative-investment performance across asset classes.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/678>) · [Paper](<https://www.ijcai.org/proceedings/2024/678>)

## Workshops (9)

### [Comparing the Impact of Financial Knowledge Graphs from Financial Reports and Wikidata in Asset Recommendation](<https://eprints.gla.ac.uk/328251/2/328251.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-guo-financial-knowledge-graphs

**Authors:** Lubingzhi Guo, Javier Sanz-Cruzado, Richard McCreadie

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Recommender Systems in Finance

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling · Alternative Data

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** financial-asset recommendation · stock ranking

**Methods:** financial knowledge graphs

**Focus:** Compares report-derived and Wikidata knowledge graphs when ranking U.S. stocks for financial-asset recommendation and monthly portfolio returns.

**Why it matters:** Shows whether structured company knowledge can improve profitable security selection beyond price and news inputs alone.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/view/fin-recsys2024/program#:~:text=Comparing%20the%20Impact%20of%20Financial%20Knowledge%20Graphs>) · [Paper](<https://eprints.gla.ac.uk/328251/2/328251.pdf>)

---

### [Examining the Effect of News Context on Algorithmic Trading](<https://aclanthology.org/2024.finnlp-2.3.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-gangopadhyay-news-algorithmic-trading

**Authors:** Surupendu Gangopadhyay, Prasenjit Majumder

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Joint Workshop of FinNLP and AgentScen

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling

**Assets / frequency:** Derivatives · Intraday

**Tasks:** algorithmic futures trading

**Methods:** proximal policy optimization · news-context embeddings

**Focus:** Trains a proximal-policy-optimization agent to combine news context with prices when choosing minute-level NIFTY 50 futures positions and quantities.

**Why it matters:** Measures whether contextual news improves realized return, drawdown, volatility, Sharpe, and Sortino outcomes for an executable trading policy.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/nlg.csie.ntu.edu.tw/finnlp-agentscen/accepted-papers#:~:text=Examining%20the%20Effect%20of%20News%20Context>) · [Paper](<https://aclanthology.org/2024.finnlp-2.3.pdf>)

---

### [FAR-Trans: An Investment Dataset for Financial Asset Recommendation](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08692>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-sanzcruzado-far-trans

**Authors:** Javier Sanz-Cruzado, Nikolaos Droukas, Richard McCreadie

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Recommender Systems in Finance

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Evaluation · Alpha Modeling

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Tasks:** financial-asset recommendation · recommender benchmarking

**Methods:** recommender systems

**Datasets:** FAR-Trans

**Identifiers:** arXiv 2407.08692

**Focus:** Releases retail-investor transactions, security prices, and investor profiles with benchmarks that rank assets for future purchase and top-recommendation portfolio returns.

**Why it matters:** Provides a public decision-facing benchmark for comparing financial recommenders on both investor behavior and realized portfolio profitability.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/view/fin-recsys2024/program#:~:text=FAR-Trans%3A%20An%20Investment%20Dataset>) · [Paper](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08692>)

---

### [GPT-Signal: Generative AI for Semi-automated Feature Engineering in the Alpha Research Process](<https://aclanthology.org/2024.finnlp-2.4.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-wang-gpt-signal

**Authors:** Yining Wang, Jinman Zhao, Yuri Lawryshyn

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Joint Workshop of FinNLP and AgentScen

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** alpha mining · stock-return prediction

**Methods:** large language models · formulaic alpha signals

**Focus:** Uses GPT-4 to generate formulaic alpha features and tests their ability to predict future returns and time buy and sell decisions for S&amp;P 500 stocks.

**Why it matters:** Automates part of factor discovery while judging the generated signals by benchmark-relative investment performance.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/nlg.csie.ntu.edu.tw/finnlp-agentscen/accepted-papers#:~:text=GPT-Signal%3A%20Generative%20AI%20for%20Semi-automated%20Feature%20Engineering>) · [Paper](<https://aclanthology.org/2024.finnlp-2.4.pdf>)

---

### [LLM-Driven Knowledge Enhancement for Securities Index Prediction](<https://ceur-ws.org/Vol-3818/paper6.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-di-llm-securities-index

**Authors:** Zaiyuan Di, Jianting Chen, Yunxiao Yang, Ling Ding, Yang Xiang

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Poster

**Subvenue:** The First International OpenKG Workshop on Large Knowledge-enhanced Models

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling · Financial Forecasting

**Assets / frequency:** Equities · Daily

**Tasks:** securities-index trend prediction · market backtesting

**Methods:** large language models · heterogeneous graph neural networks

**Datasets:** Shanghai Stock Exchange indices and related market indicators

**Identifiers:** OpenReview YYOgsrZjFz

**Focus:** Combines language-model-derived market relations with daily index data in a heterogeneous graph to predict Shanghai Stock Exchange index trends and backtest the signals.

**Why it matters:** Tests whether inexpensive knowledge extraction can improve investable index-direction forecasts in a real market backtest.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://lkm2024.openkg.org/#accepted-papers>) · [Paper](<https://ceur-ws.org/Vol-3818/paper6.pdf>)

---

### [Risk Propensity-specific Portfolio Recommendation via Self-supervised Learning](<https://drive.google.com/uc?id=1vIqj9DIiyWYHQ_WmropQGOZYfbBmfd2P&export=download>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-kim-risk-propensity-portfolio

**Authors:** Namhyoung Kim, Seung Eun Ock, Jae Wook Song

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Recommender Systems in Finance

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Asset Allocation · Risk Management

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** risk-profile portfolio recommendation · momentum portfolio construction

**Methods:** self-supervised learning · risk-based asset clustering

**Focus:** Clusters Korean equities by volatility and tail-risk measures before constructing momentum portfolios matched to an investor's risk propensity.

**Why it matters:** Connects personalized recommendations to explicit return, volatility, Sharpe, drawdown, and cumulative-performance tradeoffs.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/view/fin-recsys2024/program#:~:text=Risk%20Propensity-specific%20Portfolio%20Recommendation>) · [Paper](<https://drive.google.com/uc?id=1vIqj9DIiyWYHQ_WmropQGOZYfbBmfd2P&export=download>)

### [Sentiment trading with large language models](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19245>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-kirtac-sentiment-trading

**Authors:** Kemal Kirtac, Guido Germano

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Poster

**Subvenue:** AI4Research

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling · Alternative Data

**Assets / frequency:** Equities · Daily

**Tasks:** stock-return prediction · long-short portfolio construction

**Methods:** large language models · financial-news sentiment analysis

**Identifiers:** arXiv 2412.19245 · OpenReview 47pcnKq9Mn · DOI 10.1016/j.frl.2024.105227

**Focus:** Scores U.S. financial news with language models and forms value-weighted long, short, and self-financing long-short stock portfolios from the signals.

**Why it matters:** Demonstrates transaction-cost-aware portfolio gains and a 3.05 Sharpe ratio for the strongest language-model sentiment strategy.

**Notes:** The workshop accepted this paper as an AI4Research poster; the DOI identifies its 2024 Finance Research Letters publication.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://ai4research.github.io/#:~:text=Sentiment%20trading%20with%20large%20language%20models>) · [Paper](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19245>)

---

### [Stock Recommendations for Individual Investors: A Temporal Graph Network Approach with Mean-variance Efficient Learning](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.07223>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-lee-stock-recommendations

**Authors:** Youngbin Lee, Yejin Kim, Javier Sanz-Cruzado, Richard McCreadie, Yongjae Lee

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Recommender Systems in Finance

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Alpha Modeling · Portfolio Optimization

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** personalized stock recommendation · portfolio construction

**Methods:** temporal graph networks · mean-variance efficient sampling

**Identifiers:** arXiv 2404.07223

**Focus:** Models time-varying investor-stock interactions and mean-variance efficiency to recommend personalized equity portfolios with stronger return and Sharpe outcomes.

**Why it matters:** Aligns stock recommendations with portfolio diversification and profitability instead of optimizing recommendation relevance alone.

**Notes:** The workshop program uses Mean-variance Efficient Learning; the public manuscript and later ACM ICAIF paper use Mean-Variance Efficient Sampling, and the later ICAIF acceptance is cataloged separately.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/view/fin-recsys2024/program#:~:text=Stock%20Recommendations%20for%20Individual%20Investors>) · [Paper](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.07223>)

---

### [Wealth Guide at the FinLLM Challenge Task: A Sophisticated Language Model Solution for Financial Trading Decisions](<https://aclanthology.org/2024.finnlp-2.13.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-ijcai-das-wealth-guide

**Authors:** Sarmistha Das, R E Zera Marveen Lyngkhoi, Sriparna Saha, Alka Maurya

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Joint Workshop of FinNLP and AgentScen — FinLLM Shared Task

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Financial Agents · Alpha Modeling

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** single-stock trading · buy-sell-hold decision making

**Methods:** large language models

**Focus:** Maps stock and exchange-traded-fund prices plus financial news to explicit buy, sell, or hold actions in the FinLLM single-stock-trading task.

**Why it matters:** Demonstrates a language-model trading policy whose winning task result is evaluated with realized Sharpe ratio rather than text accuracy alone.

**Notes:** The organizer acceptance list shortens the title to Wealth Guide: A Sophisticated Language Model Solution for Financial Trading Decisions; this record uses the exact ACL proceedings title.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://sites.google.com/nlg.csie.ntu.edu.tw/finnlp-agentscen/accepted-papers#:~:text=Wealth%20Guide%3A%20A%20Sophisticated%20Language%20Model>) · [Paper](<https://aclanthology.org/2024.finnlp-2.13.pdf>)
